is a [Malayalam language](/wiki/Malayalam_language "Malayalam language") [free to air](/wiki/Free-to-air "Free-to-air") television channel owned by the owners of the [Malayalam](/wiki/Malayalam "Malayalam") [daily newspaper](/wiki/Daily_newspaper "Daily newspaper") Kerala Kaumudi. The channel was officially launched on 5 May 2013. Its main program is [Tom and Jessy.](/w/index.php?title=Tom_and_Jessy.&action=edit&redlink=1 "Tom and Jessy. (page does not exist)") [Prime Minister](/wiki/Prime_Minister "Prime Minister") [Manmohan Singh](/wiki/Manmohan_Singh "Manmohan Singh") had already unveiled the logo of the channel a year earlier during the centenary celebrations of Kerala Kaumudi. The program menu comprises news, talks, interviews and entertainment.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

Kerala Kaumudi

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Type | Daily newspaper[[1]](#cite_note-1) |
| Format | [Broadsheet](/wiki/Broadsheet "Broadsheet") |
| Publisher | Darshan Ravi |
| Editor-in-chief | Deepu Ravi |
| Founded | 1911; 115 years ago (1911) |
| Language | [Malayalam](/wiki/Malayalam "Malayalam") |
| Headquarters | Kaumudi Buildings, [Thiruvananthapuram](/wiki/Thiruvananthapuram "Thiruvananthapuram") – 695 024, India |
| Website | [www.keralakaumudi.com](http://www.keralakaumudi.com/) |
| Free online archives | [www.keralakaumudi.com/news/print/index.htm](http://www.keralakaumudi.com/news/print/index.htm) |

***Kerala Kaumudi*** is a [Malayalam language](/wiki/Malayalam "Malayalam") daily newspaper published from [Kerala](/wiki/Kerala "Kerala"), [India](/wiki/India "India"), headquartered in the state capital, [Thiruvananthapuram](/wiki/Thiruvananthapuram "Thiruvananthapuram"). It was founded in 1911 by [C. V. Kunhiraman](/wiki/C._V._Kunhiraman "C. V. Kunhiraman") as a periodical. His son [K. Sukumaran](/wiki/K._Sukumaran_(journalist) "K. Sukumaran (journalist)") later served as the newspaper's editor. Kerala Kaumudi is one of the largest circulated newspapers in Malayalam with 9 editions, published from [Thiruvananthapuram](/wiki/Thiruvananthapuram "Thiruvananthapuram"), [Kollam](/wiki/Kollam "Kollam"), [Alappuzha](/wiki/Alappuzha "Alappuzha"), [Pathanamthitta](/wiki/Pathanamthitta "Pathanamthitta"), [Kottayam](/wiki/Kottayam "Kottayam"), [Kochi](/wiki/Kochi "Kochi"), [Thrissur](/wiki/Thrissur "Thrissur"), [Kozhikode](/wiki/Kozhikode "Kozhikode") and [Kannur](/wiki/Kannur "Kannur") besides being circulated in the [United Arab Emirates](/wiki/United_Arab_Emirates "United Arab Emirates").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

In the course of over a century, Kerala Kaumudi has diversified and expanded into a multitude of media platforms.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] In addition to the daily, *Kerala Kaumudi* comprises a midday named Kerala *Kaumudi Flash*, the *Kerala Kaumudi Weekly*, the children's magazine *Magic Slate*, the glossy film magazine *Flash Movies*, an online edition of the newspaper and the television channel *Kaumudy TV*.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] *Kerala Kaumudi Weekly*, formerly edited by [K. Balakrishnan](/wiki/K._Balakrishnan "K. Balakrishnan"), remained a popular literary journal in Kerala for a long period.

## Kaumudy TV

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kerala_Kaumudi&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Kaumudy TV")]

[![Logo used since 2013](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/7/74/Kaumudy_TV_Logo.jpg/120px-Kaumudy_TV_Logo.jpg)](/wiki/File:Kaumudy_TV_Logo.jpg "Logo used since 2013")

Logo used since 2013

**Kaumudy TV** is a [Malayalam language](/wiki/Malayalam_language "Malayalam language") [free to air](/wiki/Free-to-air "Free-to-air") television channel owned by the owners of the [Malayalam](/wiki/Malayalam "Malayalam") [daily newspaper](/wiki/Daily_newspaper "Daily newspaper") Kerala Kaumudi. The channel was officially launched on 5 May 2013. Its main program is [Tom and Jessy.](/w/index.php?title=Tom_and_Jessy.&action=edit&redlink=1 "Tom and Jessy. (page does not exist)") [Prime Minister](/wiki/Prime_Minister "Prime Minister") [Manmohan Singh](/wiki/Manmohan_Singh "Manmohan Singh") had already unveiled the logo of the channel a year earlier during the centenary celebrations of Kerala Kaumudi. The program menu comprises news, talks, interviews and entertainment.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Editions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kerala_Kaumudi&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Editions")]

*Kerala Kaumudi* is printed from [Thiruvananthapuram](/wiki/Thiruvananthapuram "Thiruvananthapuram"), [Kollam](/wiki/Kollam "Kollam"), [Alappuzha](/wiki/Alappuzha "Alappuzha"), [Kottayam](/wiki/Kottayam "Kottayam"), [Kochi](/wiki/Kochi "Kochi"), [Pathanamthitta](/wiki/Pathanamthitta "Pathanamthitta"), [Thrissur](/wiki/Thrissur "Thrissur"), [Kozhikode](/wiki/Kozhikode "Kozhikode"), [Kannur](/wiki/Kannur "Kannur") in Kerala and [Bangalore](/wiki/Bangalore "Bangalore").

## See also

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kerala_Kaumudi&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: See also")]

* [Kaumudy TV](/wiki/Kaumudy_TV "Kaumudy TV")
